what_it_does: Defines the Pydantic schemas, serialization builder, and ui:// address grammar/parser/normalizer/validator that project both corpus and live UI registry entries into a shared union record shape.
observations: SCHEMA_VER = 3 with additive, versioned field changes documented in comments.
Capabilities is an opt-in bool-object model for UI affordances.
UiComponentEntry carries ref, kind, title, capabilities, optional dom_handle/camera_ref, and version, with camelCase aliases for wire output.
build_ui_info rejects non-UiComponentEntry values and duplicate refs, dumping entries by alias.
A permissive regex parses ui:// addresses into segments and an optional state.
normalize_capabilities maps list/dict/Capabilities inputs to the canonical bool-object and raises on unknown capability strings.
UnionAddressRecord merges corpus fields (represents, code, tier, howto, page) with live fields (kind, title, capabilities).
from_corpus derives kind from region: canvas stays canvas, everything else becomes chrome.
from_live handles two row forms: bare refs (ui://<kind>/<ref>) and full-string S1 refs (ui://...).
validate_address_record returns enumerated problems instead of raising on the first failure.
conform_corpus and conform_live produce {records, problems} maps for their respective inputs.
v3 adds an optional page field binding a rendered HTML face to an address.
standouts: Explicit separation between the permissive structural ui:// grammar and the stricter record-level validation.
Dual projection paths from_corpus and from_live that reconcile two different registry conventions into one union record.
Fail-loud enumerated validation that collects every problem rather than stopping at the first.
conventions: Pydantic BaseModel with snake_case Python fields and camelCase aliases for JSON output.
Schema-additive evolution: new fields carry defaults so older consumers still validate.
Fail loud with TypeError/ValueError for bad entries, duplicate refs, unknown capabilities, and malformed addresses.
Heavy inline design-rationale comments referencing rules and external guide sections.
concerns: from_corpus maps any non-canvas region to kind chrome, which may misclassify corpus regions that semantically represent field/panel/ext.
conform_live unpacks a sixth extras_dict position but never reads or validates it.
The page field is declared as dict | None but its exact runtime shape is only described in comments, not enforced by a nested model.
notes: This is the contract layer behind ui:// addressing: it defines what the UI registry serves and how two different address conventions are parsed, normalized, and validated into one record shape.
questions: What is the exact shape and consumer of the optional sixth extras_dict in live UI_REGISTRY rows?
How does the frontend resolve and render the optional page-as-a-face binding on a separate origin?
Where are Suite.UI_REGISTRY and runtime/page_face.py defined relative to this file?
Should corpus regions other than canvas ever map to kinds other than chrome?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to define the UI registry serialization shape and the ui:// addressing contract; the code matches by implementing models, builder, parser, normalizer, and validators for both corpus and live registries.
apparent_themes: Structural grammar separated from semantic record
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a single, versioned, additive contract so the backend UI registry and frontend UI resolver can address components with one ui:// grammar while each side keeps its native record shape.
novelty: The dual projection classmethods from_corpus and from_live map two different address conventions into one union record, and the explicit split between a permissive structural grammar and a stricter record-level validation.
contribution: It contributes versioned Pydantic schemas for UI component entries and capabilities, a JSON builder that rejects bad entries and duplicate refs, and a parser/normalizer/validator for ui:// addresses that projects both corpus and live registry rows into a common record shape.
summary_for_embedding: company/contracts/ui_info.py defines the Pydantic schemas, serialization builder, and ui:// address parser/normalizer/validator that project corpus and live UI registry entries into a shared union record shape.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to provide a stable, additive contract between backend UI registry serialization and frontend UI addressing, with explicit validation that reconciles two existing address conventions.

symbols.does: Pydantic model declaring opt-in boolean UI affordances.
symbols.description: Defines pointable, spotlit, presentable, openable, and drivenReadOnly as False-by-default fields; this is the canonical capability shape used by the frontend and address records.

symbols.does: Pydantic model for one UI component in the served registry.
symbols.description: Holds ref, kind, title, capabilities, optional dom_handle/camera_ref, and version; emits camelCase aliases for wire serialization and is the per-entry shape that build_ui_info validates and dumps.

symbols.does: Serialize a list of UiComponentEntry objects into a ref-keyed dict.
symbols.description: Iterates entries, rejects non-UiComponentEntry values and duplicate refs, and returns a JSON-ready dict keyed by ref with fields dumped by alias.

symbols.does: Parse a ui:// string into segments and an optional state.
symbols.description: Uses a permissive regex to extract one or more path segments and an optional trailing state; raises on malformed or non-string input.

symbols.does: Convert list/dict/Capabilities inputs into the canonical bool-object.
symbols.description: Maps corpus capability strings such as driven and driven-read-only to drivenReadOnly, ensures every canonical field is present defaulting to False, and raises on unknown capability names.

symbols.does: Pydantic model unifying corpus and live address records.
symbols.description: Contains address, kind, region, capabilities, and optional join fields (represents, code, states, tier, title, howto, page); provides classmethods from_corpus and from_live to project each side's native record into this shared shape.

symbols.does: Project a corpus addresses.json entry into a UnionAddressRecord.
symbols.description: Parses the address, derives kind from region (canvas stays canvas, otherwise chrome), normalizes capabilities, and copies optional corpus fields like represents, code, tier, title, howto, and page.

symbols.does: Project a live UI_REGISTRY row into a UnionAddressRecord.
symbols.description: Handles both bare-ref rows (constructs ui://<kind>/<ref>) and full-string S1 rows (ref already ui://...), parses the address, sets region accordingly, and normalizes capabilities.

symbols.does: Return a list of conformance problems for a UnionAddressRecord.
symbols.description: Checks that the address parses, kind is in ADDRESS_KINDS, region is non-empty, and capability keys are canonical; returns problems rather than raising to allow enumeration.

symbols.does: Validate a corpus addresses.json map against the grammar and record shape.
symbols.description: Projects each entry to UnionAddressRecord via from_corpus, collects validation problems, and returns a dict with records and problems.

symbols.does: Validate a live UI_REGISTRY iterable against the grammar and record shape.
symbols.description: Unpacks each row's ref/kind/title/handle/caps, projects via from_live, collects validation problems, and returns records keyed by canonical address plus problems.
